note: The single source of truth for WHY each file is omitted from the CPU-only line-coverage gate and WHERE it is exercised instead. Every glob in [tool.coverage.run].omit and [tool.coverage.report].omit in pyproject.toml MUST have exactly one row here, and every row MUST match a live omit glob — tests/test_exclusions_ledger_drift.py fails the build on any drift. 'exercised_by' names the CI workflow (or an explicit tracked gap) that DOES cover the file; it is documentation, not a machine contract.

exclusions.reason: Julia accelerator wrappers require an optional Julia runtime. Python dispatcher fallthrough is covered in the release suite, and the Julia tier is exercised through the optional julia extra.
exclusions.exercised_by: local [julia] extra (juliacall + Julia runtime); no CI lane — installing Julia adds ~4 min JIT boot per run. Tracked gap: the Julia wrapper branches are not CI-covered, only the Python dispatcher fallthrough is.

exclusions.reason: Provider HAL adapters require vendor SDKs, credentials, accounts, or live resources. Contract and conformance tests cover injected-provider behavior; live branches remain in provider smoke/readiness workflows.
exclusions.exercised_by: provider-isolated-smoke.yml (injected-provider contract/conformance); live SDK branches remain a tracked gap pending PROVIDER-DEPTH real runs.

exclusions.reason: Provider capability discovery depends on optional vendor SDK presence and live provider metadata. Release behavior is covered by discovery contract tests with injected snapshots and provider fixtures.
exclusions.exercised_by: provider-isolated-smoke.yml (discovery contract tests with injected snapshots).

exclusions.reason: Provider smoke execution is an operational live-provider entry point. It is governed by hardware-marked smoke workflows rather than the CPU-only unit-test line gate.

IBM latency probing submits or inspects live backend jobs and requires operator-approved credentials. It is governed by preregistration and result-pack evidence instead of ordinary unit-test coverage.
exclusions.exercised_by: none — operational live-IBM entry point gated by preregistration + result-pack evidence. Tracked gap by design (no CI credentials).

exclusions.reason: OpenPulse control paths require provider-specific pulse support and live hardware constraints. Feasibility and adapter contracts cover offline behavior; live execution remains hardware-marked.
exclusions.exercised_by: none for live pulse execution — offline feasibility/adapter contracts covered in the core suite; live branches are a tracked hardware gap.

exclusions.reason: S1 IBM feedback submission is a campaign submitter with live-provider side effects. Release evidence is preregistration, manifest, and result-pack based.
exclusions.exercised_by: none — campaign submitter with live side effects; evidence is preregistration + manifest + result-pack. Tracked gap by design.

exclusions.reason: GPU batch VQE depends on optional accelerator runtimes that are not part of the pinned CPU-only release lane. Dispatcher and fallback behavior are covered by core accelerator tests.
exclusions.exercised_by: differentiable-frameworks.yml optional-GPU-contract job + tests/test_gpu_batch_vqe*.py (dispatcher/fallback in the core suite).

exclusions.reason: JAX NQS requires optional JAX accelerator surfaces. Contract tests cover import and fallback behavior while accelerator-specific execution remains in optional runtime lanes.
MPS evolution depends on optional tensor-network runtime surfaces. Core contraction and MPS baseline behavior are covered elsewhere in the release suite.
exclusions.exercised_by: tests/test_mps_evolution.py under the optional tensor-network extra; core contraction baseline is in the release suite.

exclusions.reason: The JAX autodiff Kuramoto base tier needs jax[cuda]/jax[cpu], absent from the pinned CPU-only CI lane. Its importorskip test runs locally (GPU) or in the differentiable-frameworks overlay lane.
exclusions.exercised_by: differentiable-frameworks.yml full profile (importorskip); dispatcher fallthrough is covered in the core suite.

exclusions.reason: The JAX autodiff delayed-Kuramoto tier needs jax[cuda]/jax[cpu], absent from the pinned CPU-only CI lane. Its importorskip test runs locally (GPU) or in the differentiable-frameworks overlay lane.
exclusions.exercised_by: differentiable-frameworks.yml full profile (importorskip); dispatcher fallthrough is covered in the core suite.

exclusions.reason: The JAX autodiff Kuramoto-MPC tier needs jax[cuda]/jax[cpu], absent from the pinned CPU-only CI lane. Its importorskip test runs locally (GPU) or in the differentiable-frameworks overlay lane.
exclusions.exercised_by: differentiable-frameworks.yml full profile (importorskip); dispatcher fallthrough is covered in the core suite.

exclusions.reason: Mitiq integration depends on optional external mitigation dependencies. Integration behavior is covered by dedicated Mitiq tests when the mitigation extra is installed.

Quantum persistent homology has optional topological dependencies and hardware-loop branches. Offline contracts are covered by topology analysis and optimizer tests; live branches remain hardware-marked.
exclusions.exercised_by: topology-analysis + optimizer tests (offline contracts); live QPU loops are a tracked hardware gap.

exclusions.reason: The Kuramoto neural operator forecaster depends on the optional torch surrogate stack, absent from the CPU-only line gate. Host-independent arithmetic is factored into covered modules.
exclusions.exercised_by: forecasting suite under the optional [torch] extra; the cost-model arithmetic in neural_operator_cost_model.py is NOT omitted and is covered to 100%.

exclusions.reason: The advantage study orchestrates the torch surrogate end-to-end; its test skips without the optional torch extra, so its lines are excluded from the CPU-only line gate.
exclusions.exercised_by: tests/test_neural_operator_advantage.py under the optional [torch] extra; the host-independent arithmetic lives in neural_operator_cost_model.py, which is NOT omitted and is covered to 100%.
